articles.title: My Inferentia port matched its reference token-for-token — and still output garbage
articles.description: Porting Gemma-4 31B (dense) to AWS Inferentia2: the tensor-parallel recipe that worked at 12B collapses at 31B, NxD ModelBuilder saves it — and then a passing validation lies to your face.
articles.author: xbill

articles.title: Five Gemma-4 models, one accelerator: what porting E2B 31B to AWS Inferentia2 taught me
articles.description: A side-by-side of all five Gemma-4 variants on Inferentia2 — PLE, KV-sharing, MatFormer, mixed attention, and a 128-expert MoE — the recipe that evolved to carry all of them, and the one bug that shows up in every single one.
articles.author: xbill

articles.title: Porting Gemma-4 12B (the encoder-free multimodal one) to AWS Inferentia2
articles.description: The 12B ships as a multimodal class with no encoder loaded, and its sliding-window attention overflows Neuron's fused-attention SBUF. Three surgical fixes and it serves Paris at ~15 tok/s on one inf2.8xlarge.
articles.author: xbill

articles.title: Porting a 128-expert MoE (Gemma-4 26B-A4B) to AWS Inferentia2 — where every rank weighted the wrong experts
articles.description: The MoE was the hard one: a dual-path FFN, a sparse expert loop that won't trace, and a bug where the device output was empty while the CPU reference was perfect and every unit test passed.
